question: The term 'precious stones' refers to ________.
choices: traditional patterns sewn into cloth using coloured thread
valuable jewels such as diamonds used in decoration
long, thin strings used in weaving and embroidery
soft cloth produced naturally by a silkworm

question: The word 'dyes' refers to ________.
choices: traditional designs made on fabric using thin threads
valuable jewels worn as accessories
things you can use to change the colour of clothes
patterns that have regular geometric shapes

question: The word 'heritage' refers to ________.
choices: a type of embroidery design used only in celebration events
the process of making cloth from natural materials
a method of producing silk using traditional tools
traditional beliefs and ideas that have been in a society for a long time

question: The word 'embroidery' refers to ________.
choices: decorative designs sewn onto fabric using thread
a type of traditional music played at cultural events
the process of weaving silk using natural dyes
a pattern printed onto fabric using a special machine

question: If an object is described as 'hollow', it means it is ________.
choices: very hard and impossible to break under pressure
empty inside, not solid
shaped like a perfect circle or sphere
covered in bright and vibrant patterns

question: Choose the correct preposition: 'She enjoyed the special effects. _____ addition, she loved the music.'
choices: On
At
In
By

question: The phrase 'on her part' is closest in meaning to ________.
choices: in her opinion about the matter
as far as the story is concerned
from the audience's perspective
done by her / her responsibility

question: Choose the correct phrase to complete the sentence: 'She has been practising every day, so _____, she will pass the audition.'
choices: in all likelihood
at least
under the impression
by accident

question: 'Obviously, navigators in ancient times were unable to use GPS.' Choose the correct past modal form.
choices: Navigators must have used GPS in ancient times
Navigators couldn't have used GPS in ancient times
Navigators might have used GPS in ancient times
Navigators would have used GPS in ancient times

question: 'I assume there would have been much simpler ways of making cloth in the past.' What does this modal form express?
choices: a negative deduction about what happened in the past
a certainty that something definitely happened
an assumption or logical conclusion about the past
an impossibility — something that could not have occurred

question: Choose the correct past modal to complete: 'The ivory comb _____ for royalty in the past — it was far too elaborate for ordinary people.'
choices: might be made
can't have been made
could being made
must have been made

question: 'I'm pretty sure this artefact was very old.' Choose the correct past modal to rewrite this sentence.
choices: This artefact must have been very old
This artefact might not have been very old
This artefact can't have been very old
This artefact would be very old

question: Choose the correct reduced form of: 'I left the cinema, and I wanted to know more about the main actor.'
choices: Having left the cinema, I wanted to know more
Left the cinema, I wanted to know more
I left the cinema, wanting to know more about the main actor
Not leaving the cinema, I wanted to know more

question: Choose the correct reduced form of: 'Adel left his bag at home, which left him without any money.'
choices: Adel left his bag at home, having left him without any money
Having left his bag at home, Adel was without any money
Adel left his bag at home, leaving him without any money
Left his bag at home, Adel had no money

question: Choose the correct reduced form of: 'After I had finished my work, I decided to have a break.'
choices: Finishing my work, I decided to have a break
After finishing my work, I decided to have a break
I finished my work, deciding to have a break
Having finished my work, I decided to have a break

question: 'This new play is a ________ start to the city's cultural weekend.' (describes someone/something with a lot of energy)
choices: lively
alive
tasteful
classic

question: 'I quite like some ________ music, especially pieces by Beethoven and Mozart.' (related to traditional forms of art/music)
choices: comical
classical
historic
childlike

question: 'The restaurant offered ________ snacks during the interval of the performance.' (food with a good taste)
choices: lively
classic
tasty
vibrant

question: The word 'comical' can be placed ________ the noun it describes.
choices: only before
only after
only in the middle of a sentence
before or after

question: The word 'episode' refers to ________.
choices: a single instalment of a TV or radio series
a comedy series featuring the same actors in every show
the group of actors who perform in a play or film
the main storyline of a play or book

question: The word 'season' in the context of TV shows refers to ________.
choices: a special episode broadcast only on public holidays
a set of episodes forming one complete series of a TV show
the time of year when new films are released in cinemas
a live comedy performance held at a specific time of year

question: The phrase 'be a must-watch' means ________.
choices: something that is boring and not worth watching
something that requires preparation before watching
something you cannot afford to miss
something that is only suitable for a small audience

question: To 'heckle somebody' at a performance means to ________.
choices: applaud enthusiastically at the end of the show
follow the performer closely after the event
offer helpful feedback to improve the performance
shout rude or mocking comments at a performer during the show

question: Among these adjectives describing art and colour, which one is NEGATIVE?
choices: insipid
distinctive
vibrant
subtle

question: The adjective 'distinctive' is used to describe something that ________.
choices: is easy to see clearly from a distance
has a unique quality that makes it stand out
uses only natural materials in its creation
belongs to an old and well-respected tradition

question: The adjective 'subdued' describes colours or designs that are ________.
choices: extremely bright and attention-grabbing
full of energy and life
quiet, not bright, and lacking intensity
made from traditional natural materials

question: The adjective 'clashing' is used to describe colours that ________.
choices: blend perfectly to create a harmonious design
remind the viewer of ancient traditional patterns
represent opposite ideas or emotions in art
look unpleasant together because they do not match

question: The adjective 'cheesy' is used to describe something that is ________.
choices: overly sentimental and lacking in quality or originality
shockingly bad in a way that upsets the audience
deeply offensive and inappropriate for public viewing
impossible to believe because of poor acting

question: A 'lively' show or performance is one that ________.
choices: contains serious themes that make the audience reflect
is full of energy, excitement, and keeps the audience engaged
uses only classical music and traditional costumes
is intended for very young children and families

question: An 'offensive' performance is one that ________.
choices: lacks excitement and fails to engage the audience
is far too long and makes the audience lose interest
causes anger or hurt because of rude or inappropriate content
uses old-fashioned language that audiences find hard to follow

question: Which of the following is used when MAKING A SUGGESTION in informal negotiation?
choices: I could go along with that
I don't know if I would fancy that
I can see what you're saying, but
How would you feel about changing the schedule?

question: Which of the following expressions is used to ACCEPT a suggestion during informal negotiation?
choices: I could go along with that. Fair enough.
I don't want to be awkward, but
What kind of thing were you thinking of?
Isn't that a bit unprofessional?

question: According to information about Jordan's parliament, when a bill is passed by more than two thirds of the Senate and House of Deputies, it ________.
choices: is automatically sent back to the Prime Minister for review
must be approved by the King before it can become law
becomes an Act of Parliament, which is a new law
is suspended and returned to the House of Deputies again

question: Jordan's parliament has ________ seats in total, with ________ reserved for women.
choices: 80 seats / 12 reserved
40 seats / 10 reserved
100 seats / 20 reserved
138 seats / 18 reserved

question: According to information about Jordan's parliament building, Jordan's independence was declared in ________.
choices: 1952 in the current parliament building in Amman
1947 in the Museum of Parliamentary Life
1978 in a ceremony at the Senate building
1946 in the building that is now the Museum of Parliamentary Life

question: At the local level, Jordan is divided into ________ governorates.
According to the article on resilience, when going through a tough time, it is important to ________.
choices: stop all activities and focus only on rest
take care of yourself by eating well, getting enough sleep, and exercising
avoid sharing problems with anyone to stay strong
take on as many tasks as possible to keep your mind busy

question: According to the article on resilience, when building resilience you should accept that ________.
choices: most problems in life are caused by other people's mistakes
younger people are naturally more resilient than older people
change happens and you cannot always control it
asking for help is a sign of weakness that should be avoided

question: The poem 'Stopping by Woods on a Snowy Evening' was part of a collection called 'New Hampshire', which won Robert Frost ________.
choices: the Nobel Prize in Literature
the Booker Prize for Fiction
the Man Booker International Prize
his first Pulitzer Prize

question: Read the following excerpt from an article about spoilers: 'We should take into account how much the Internet has changed our viewing habits. Gone are the days when everyone would watch the same thing at the same time and then talk about it the next day. Now, everyone is watching at different times across the world, and a lot of the discussion is happening in online communities.' According to this text, the Internet has changed viewing habits by ________.
choices: making people less interested in discussing what they watch
allowing people to watch the same content at the same time globally
reducing the number of online communities discussing programmes
leading to people watching at different times and discussing things online
